Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Understand the Goal: The objective is to take a highly granular event log from a manufacturing process and abstract it into a smaller set of high-level process steps. I need to provide the high-level steps, the grouping rationale, and proposed names in a structured format.

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Analyze the Input Data: Attributes: CaseID, Timestamp, Activity, Resource, AdditionalInfo. Cases provided: A1, B2. Both follow the exact same sequence of low-level activities. Sequence of Activities per case: 1. Retrieve raw metal sheet Operator A 2. Scan barcode Operator A 3. Place metal sheet on table Operator A 4. Align metal sheet Robot Arm 2 5. Preheat metal sheet Heating Unit 1 6. Pick up welding tool Operator B 7. Weld corner A Operator B 8. Weld corner B Operator B 9. Measure weld integrity Quality Sensor 1 10. Apply protective coating Coating Unit 3 11. Dry coating Heating Unit 2 12. Visual check Operator C

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Identify Natural Breakpoints Groupings: Group 1 Events 1-5: Retrieve, Scan, Place, Align, Preheat. Theme: Getting the raw material, identifying it, positioning it, and getting it ready for the main operation. Resources: Operator A, Robot Arm 2, Heating Unit 1. Candidate Name: Material Preparation Material Handling and Setup. Group 2 Events 6-8: Pick up tool, Weld A, Weld B. Theme: The core transformation shaping process. Resources: Operator B. Candidate Name: Assembly Welding Primary Fabrication. Group 3 Event 9: Measure weld integrity. Theme: Checking the quality of the immediate previous step. Group 4 Events 10-11: Apply coating, Dry coating. Theme: Surface treatment finishing. Group 5 Event 12: Visual check. Theme: Final sign-off.

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Refine and Validate Groupings against Instructions: Identify High-Level Steps: Done 5 steps identified. Justify Groupings: Group 1: Temporal proximity, shared goal of making the part ready for action, transition from manual to automated setup. Group 2: Resource continuity Operator B, logical sequence pick tool before welding, core value-add step. Group 3: Automated validation immediately following the manual task. Group 4: Specific physical chemical modification of the surface. Group 5: Human verification, final gate. Name the High-Level Activities: 1. Material Preparation and Setup 2. Welding and Assembly 3. Automated Quality Inspection 4. Surface Treatment and Finishing 5. Final Quality Control

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Draft the Output Structure: Create a clear, structured representation. For each high-level activity, include: High-Level Activity Name Included Low-Level Events Rationale Justification

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - PE
Text: Review and Polish Self-Correction during drafting: Critique: Is Assembly the right word for welding? Welding is often part of fabrication or assembly. Welding and Assembly works well. Critique: Should the visual check be grouped with the automated quality check? No, they check different things weld integrity vs overall surface finish and occur at different points intermediate vs final. Keeping them separate shows the process flow better. Format: Use Markdown tables or bulleted lists for readability. A hierarchical list with bold headers works best.
